data.title: SaneBox Tutorial: Tame Your Inbox in 5 Simple Steps
data.excerpt: Learn how to set up SaneBox step by step and reclaim hours wasted on email. This AI tool filters, prioritizes, and organizes your inbox automatically. Start your free trial today.

$media.alt: SaneBox Tutorial: Tame Your Inbox in 5 Simple Steps

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*

I used to spend my first working hour every morning sorting emails. Newsletters I never read. CC chains that had nothing to do with me. Promotional blasts from a store I visited once in 2019. The noise was relentless.
Then I tried
SaneBox

, and the math changed overnight. According to SaneBox's own data, users save 3 to 4 hours per week on email management. I can confirm: that number is real. Within two weeks, my inbox went from a stress-inducing flood to a focused list of messages that actually required my attention.

If you've ever opened Gmail, seen 347 unread messages, and quietly closed the tab, this tutorial is for you. I'll walk you through exactly how to set up SaneBox, configure its best features, and start reclaiming your time today.
Ready to stop drowning in email? Try SaneBox free for 14 days — no credit card needed.
What Is SaneBox and How Does It Work?
SaneBox

is an AI-powered email management tool that sits between you and your inbox. It works with virtually every email provider and client — Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, Yahoo, and more — without requiring you to download anything or change your email address.

Here's the core idea: SaneBox's artificial intelligence studies your email habits. It learns who you reply to, which newsletters you actually open, what gets ignored, and which senders matter. Then it automatically sorts incoming mail into smart folders so your main inbox only shows what deserves your immediate attention.
The beauty is that nothing changes on your end. You still use your same email app. SaneBox just makes it smarter behind the scenes.
Step 1: Sign Up and Connect Your Email Account
Getting started takes about 60 seconds. Head to
sanebox.com

and create your account. You'll enter your email address, and SaneBox will ask for permission to manage your mail. This works through OAuth for Gmail and standard IMAP connections for other providers.
No passwords are stored.
SaneBox never sees or stores your email credentials. It connects through secure protocols, and you can revoke access anytime from your email provider's settings.
Once connected, SaneBox starts analyzing your existing email history. It looks at:
Who you've emailed in the past 6 months
Which messages you opened, replied to, or archived
Your folder structure and labeling habits
Patterns in senders, subjects, and frequency
This initial scan happens quietly in the background. You can keep working while it runs.
Step 2: Choose Your Features on the Dashboard
After connecting, you'll land on the SaneBox dashboard at sanebox.com/dashboard. This is your control center. Navigate to the
Folders page
to see all available features — these are the smart folders and behaviors SaneBox can enable.
Each feature is a toggle. Turn it on to activate it. Here's what you'll see:
@SaneLater
— The hero feature. Low-priority emails get moved here automatically. Your inbox stays clean.
@SaneNoReplies
— Tracks emails you sent that never got a response. Perfect for follow-ups.
@SaneNews
— Newsletters and mailing lists land here instead of cluttering your inbox.
@SaneCC
— Emails where you're CC'd (not the primary recipient) go to a separate folder.
@SaneBlackHole
— Drag an email here, and you'll never hear from that sender again. Ever.
@SaneDoNotDisturb
— Pause all non-urgent email for a set period. Deep work mode, activated.
@SaneReminders
— Get nudged if you haven't received a reply by a date you set.
@SaneTomorrow / @SaneNextWeek
— Snooze emails to resurface when you're ready.
SaneScreener
— New senders get screened. You decide whether they deserve inbox access.
I recommend starting with SaneLater and SaneNews. Once you see how well those work, expand from there.
Explore all SaneBox features and start your free trial here.
Step 3: Let the AI Learn (and Train It When Needed)

SaneBox's AI gets smarter the longer you use it. During the first 24 to 48 hours, it makes its best guesses based on your email history. Some emails might land in the wrong folder — and that's expected.
The training process is simple:
1. Open your @SaneLater folder (or any SaneBox folder).
2. Find an email that was sorted incorrectly.
3. Drag it back to your inbox.

That single action teaches SaneBox: "This sender is important to me." Within a few repetitions, the AI adjusts. Conversely, if something slips into your inbox that shouldn't be there, drag it to @SaneLater, and SaneBox learns the reverse.
The more you train, the better it performs. After about a week of occasional corrections, I noticed SaneBox was sorting with roughly 95% accuracy. After a month, it felt like it could read my mind.
For extra tips on getting the most out of this training phase, check out our
SaneBox tips and best practices
Step 4: Set Up the Daily Digest
One feature I genuinely love is the
Daily Digest
. Instead of checking @SaneLater throughout the day, SaneBox sends you a single summary email — once a day — listing everything it filtered out.

You can scan the digest in 30 seconds. If anything looks important, click it to move it to your inbox. If not, ignore it guilt-free. This single feature changed how I relate to low-priority email: I no longer worry about missing something, because the digest catches it all.
You can configure the digest timing in your dashboard settings. I set mine for 7:00 PM, so I review it once during my evening wrap-up and never think about it again until tomorrow.
Step 5: Use SaneBlackHole to Unsubscribe Without Unsubscribing
This might be my favorite SaneBox trick. When a promotional email annoys you, drag it to the
@SaneBlackHole
folder. SaneBox will:
1. Automatically unsubscribe you from that mailing list (when possible).
2. Move all future emails from that sender straight to trash.
3. Never bother you with them again.

It's aggressive, it's effective, and it feels fantastic. I've BlackHoled dozens of senders over the past three months, and my inbox is measurably quieter because of it. If you've ever clicked an unsubscribe link only to keep getting emails two weeks later, SaneBlackHole is the permanent fix.
New and Standout Features Worth Knowing
SaneBox keeps evolving. Here are some features that deserve your attention if you're evaluating the tool in 2024:
SaneScreener

— This is a game-changer for anyone who gets cold emails or messages from unknown senders. SaneScreener intercepts emails from people you've never interacted with and asks you: "Do you want to receive email from this person?" You approve or block with one click. No more strangers cluttering your inbox.
SaneFwd
— Need to forward an email to yourself as a reminder? SaneFwd lets you forward it to a specific SaneBox address and it gets snoozed or sorted automatically.
Custom Training Folders
— Beyond the built-in folders, you can create your own. Want a folder for emails from your accountant? Or a "Read on weekends" folder? Build it, train it, and SaneBox handles the rest.
SaneDrafts
— Drafts you started but never sent get tracked here. No more abandoned emails floating in limbo.
SaneSummary
— For long email threads, SaneSummary provides a quick overview so you can catch up without reading 47 back-and-forth messages.
SaneNotSpam
— Legitimate emails that your provider mistakenly flagged as spam? SaneNotSpam rescues them and puts them where they belong.
You can dig deeper into all the
latest SaneBox features
in our dedicated breakdown.
Who Is SaneBox Ideal For?
SaneBox isn't just for people with overflowing inboxes (though it's spectacular for that). Here's who benefits most:
Busy professionals
who receive 100+ emails daily and can't afford to manually sort everything. SaneBox handles the triage so you focus on what matters.
Freelancers and consultants
juggling multiple client conversations. The SaneNoReplies feature alone is worth the subscription — it ensures you never forget to follow up on a proposal.
Executives and managers
buried in CC chains and FYI emails. SaneCC filters those out without losing them, so your inbox reflects actual action items.
Newsletter addicts
who subscribe to everything but read selectively. SaneNews collects them all in one place, and you browse on your own schedule.
Anyone using multiple email accounts.
The Lunch plan supports two accounts, and Dinner supports four. One SaneBox subscription, multiple inboxes tamed.
Remote workers
who need to protect focus time. SaneDoNotDisturb lets you pause email interruptions during deep work blocks — a feature I use almost every afternoon.
If any of those descriptions resonate,
SaneBox is built for you
Pricing: Which Plan Should You Pick?
SaneBox offers three plans, and choosing is straightforward:
Snack
($4.99/month billed biennially) — One email account, two features. Perfect if you just want SaneLater and one other filter. This is where most people start.
Lunch
($7.99/month billed biennially) — Two email accounts, six features. The sweet spot for professionals managing a work and personal inbox. You get enough features to build a seriously organized system.
Dinner
($24.99/month billed biennially) — Four email accounts, all features included, plus phone support. Ideal for power users, executives, or anyone who wants the full toolkit across multiple accounts.
All plans include a
14-day free trial
with no credit card required. The biennial billing saves you up to 56% compared to monthly pricing, which is a substantial discount if you know you'll stick with it.
If you're unsure, start with Snack on the free trial. You can always upgrade later.
Pick your plan and start your 14-day free trial at SaneBox.
My Results After Three Months
I want to be specific about what changed, because vague claims like "it saved me time" don't help you decide.
Before SaneBox:
I opened my inbox to 80 to 120 new emails every morning. I spent 45 to 60 minutes sorting, archiving, and deleting. I missed important messages at least twice a week because they got buried.
After SaneBox:

My inbox shows 8 to 15 emails each morning — all from people I actually communicate with. The rest waits in SaneLater for my daily digest review. My sorting time dropped to about 5 minutes. I haven't missed an important email since week two.
The hidden benefit?
Reduced anxiety.
I used to dread opening email. Now it's neutral — sometimes even pleasant. That shift alone is worth the subscription.
For more real-world examples of how people use SaneBox in different professions, see our
SaneBox use cases guide
Final Thoughts: Is SaneBox Worth It?
Absolutely. SaneBox does one thing — email management — and it does it exceptionally well. The AI is accurate, the setup is effortless, and the time savings are immediate and measurable.

There's no app to download. No workflow to redesign. No learning curve that requires a weekend seminar. You connect your email, toggle a few features, and the tool goes to work. If the AI makes a mistake, you correct it with a single drag-and-drop, and it remembers.
The 14-day free trial is generous enough to see real results before you commit. And at $4.99 per month for the entry plan, the ROI is almost absurd — you'll save that in time within your first week.
Stop managing email manually. Try SaneBox free for 14 days and feel the difference yourself.

children.text: Does SaneBox work with Gmail, Outlook, and Apple Mail?

Yes. SaneBox works with every major email provider and client, including Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, Yahoo Mail, and others. It connects through secure protocols (OAuth for Gmail, IMAP for others) and requires no downloads or browser extensions.
Will SaneBox delete any of my emails?

No. SaneBox only moves emails between folders. Nothing is deleted unless you specifically use the SaneBlackHole feature, which sends future emails from that sender to trash. You can always reverse any action by moving emails back to your inbox.
How long does it take for SaneBox to learn my email habits?

SaneBox starts working immediately by analyzing your email history. Initial sorting is usually quite accurate within 24 to 48 hours. With occasional drag-and-drop corrections, most users report 95%+ accuracy within the first week, and it continues improving from there.
Can I try SaneBox before paying?

Absolutely. SaneBox offers a 14-day free trial on all plans with no credit card required. You get full access to your chosen plan's features during the trial, so you can experience the time savings before committing.
